overrides.override_summary: Franchisee solely responsible for Fast Food Act compliance (min wage, working hours, employment standards)
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchise operations may face higher labor costs than disclosed in Item 7/19 — franchisee bears all compliance risk

overrides.override_summary: No waiver of state franchise law claims including fraud in inducement; no disclaimer of reliance on franchisor statements
overrides.why_it_matters: Preserves franchisee's right to pursue claims under CA franchise law regardless of FA provisions

overrides.override_summary: Release required for renewal/transfer does NOT apply to claims under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees retain right to pursue franchise law claims even after signing renewal/transfer release

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon insolvency might not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law
overrides.why_it_matters: Provides potential protection against automatic termination in bankruptcy

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring action in MD for claims under MD franchise law (unless preempted by federal law)
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Atlanta, GA venue for MD franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: 3-year statute of limitations for claims under MD franchise law from grant of franchise
overrides.why_it_matters: Establishes specific SOL for MD franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor must indemnify MN franchisees against third-party trademark infringement claims
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees have additional protection against trademark disputes that other states' franchisees lack

overrides.override_summary: 90 days' notice of termination (60 days to cure) required per Minn. Stat. §80C.14
overrides.why_it_matters: Significantly longer cure period than FA's 10-30 days; provides more time to resolve issues
Gives MN franchisees 6 months advance notice vs FA's potentially shorter window

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require litigation outside Minnesota per Minn. Stat. §80C.21
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Atlanta, GA venue requirement entirely for MN franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require waiver of jury trial, consent to injunctive relief, liquidated damages, termination penalties, or judgment notes
overrides.why_it_matters: Voids multiple FA provisions that limit franchisee remedies and expose franchisee to penalties

overrides.override_summary: Release for renewal/sale/transfer does not apply to claims under Minn. Rule 2860.4400D
overrides.why_it_matters: Preserves MN franchise law claims through renewal/transfer events

overrides.override_summary: Release does not apply to claims under ND Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees retain franchise law rights through releases

overrides.override_summary: Non-competition covenants generally considered unenforceable in ND
overrides.why_it_matters: Post-term noncompete likely void in ND — franchisee may open competing business immediately

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring action in ND to extent required by ND Franchise Investment Law
North Dakota law applies to extent required by law (instead of Georgia law)
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchise law governs over Georgia law for applicable claims

overrides.override_summary: Assurance of Discontinuance: removed no-poach provision from FA; will not enforce in existing agreements
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees and their employees are not restricted from being recruited by other Sonic franchisees
